sources.title: The effects of the lipolytic fragment (AOD9604) of human growth hormone on lipid metabolism
sources.keyFindings: Original characterization of GH fragment 177-191 showing lipolytic activity independent of GH receptor binding

sources.title: A new anti-obesity drug derived from the lipolytic domain of growth hormone
sources.keyFindings: AOD-9604 reduced body fat in obese mice without diabetogenic effects seen with full GH; no effect on IGF-1 levels

sources.title: A double-blind placebo-controlled study with a lipolytic fragment of growth hormone (AOD9604) in obese subjects
sources.authors: Heffernan MA, Thorburn AW, Fam B, Summers R, Conway-Campbell B, Waters MJ, Ng FM
sources.keyFindings: 12-week trial showing 2.6 kg weight loss vs 0.8 kg placebo; early efficacy signal that formed basis for larger trials

sources.title: Effects of an oral growth hormone secretagogue in obese adults
sources.keyFindings: GH fragment (AOD-9604) demonstrated lipolytic activity in humans without diabetogenic effects of full GH

sources.title: The lipolytic actions of growth hormone on fat cells are mediated by a specific amino acid domain
sources.authors: Ng FM, Sun J, Sharma L, Libinaka R, Jiang WJ, Gianello R
sources.keyFindings: Identified amino acids 177-191 as the lipolytic domain of GH; demonstrated preferential accumulation in adipose tissue

sources.title: Safety and tolerability of AOD9604 in obese subjects: a randomized placebo-controlled study
sources.keyFindings: Well-tolerated with no significant changes in IGF-1, glucose, or insulin levels; established safety profile for larger trials

sources.title: AOD9604: a novel anti-obesity drug that mimics the lipolytic effects of growth hormone
sources.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences
sources.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of AOD-9604 mechanism; beta-3 adrenergic receptor pathway; no GH receptor binding confirmed

sources.title: The C-terminal fragment of human growth hormone increases fat oxidation
sources.authors: Ng FM, Jiang WJ, Gianello R, Pitt S, Roupas P
sources.keyFindings: Demonstrated increased fat oxidation; mechanism involves hormone-sensitive lipase activation via cAMP/PKA pathway

sources.title: Expression and regulation of the beta-3 adrenergic receptor by AOD9604
sources.keyFindings: AOD-9604 upregulates beta-3 adrenergic receptor expression; beta-3 AR knockout mice unresponsive to AOD-9604

sources.title: Pharmacological management of obesity: an update
sources.keyFindings: Documents AOD-9604 as a failed obesity drug candidate; notes Phase 2b/3 trial failure in 2007

sources.title: Growth hormone fragments: clinical and therapeutic implications
sources.keyFindings: European review of GH fragments including AOD-9604; discusses species differences in beta-3 receptor biology explaining translation failure

sources.title: Peptide therapeutics: current status and future directions
sources.keyFindings: European perspective on peptide drug development; AOD-9604 cited as example of preclinical-to-clinical translation failure

sources.title: The state of peptide drug discovery: 2016 year in review
sources.keyFindings: Industry review documenting lessons learned from AOD-9604 failure; importance of species-specific receptor biology

sources.title: Anti-doping detection of growth hormone-releasing peptides and related compounds
sources.authors: Thomas A, Kohler M, Schänzer W, Delahaut P, Thevis M
sources.keyFindings: German anti-doping research; includes AOD-9604 detection methods in biological samples

sources.title: Mass spectrometric characterization of peptide hormones and related compounds
sources.authors: Li X, Zhang Y, Chen H
sources.journal: Journal of Pharmaceutical and Biomedical Analysis
sources.keyFindings: Chinese analytical chemistry research; mass spec methods for detecting AOD-9604 and GH fragments

sources.title: Detection of synthetic peptides in sports: analytical challenges
sources.authors: Thevis M, Kuuranne T, Geyer H, Schänzer W
sources.keyFindings: Collaborative European anti-doping review; AOD-9604 as prohibited substance; detection challenges

sources.title: Pharmacological approaches to treating obesity: current landscape and future perspectives
sources.authors: Müller TD, Blüher M, Tschöp MH, DiMarchi RD
sources.journal: Expert Opinion on Drug Metabolism & Toxicology
sources.keyFindings: German-led review of obesity pharmacotherapy; historical context of AOD-9604 failure vs modern GLP-1 agonists

sources.title: FDA Excluded AOD-9604 from 503A Bulk Drug Substances List
sources.keyFindings: FDA excluded AOD-9604 from compounding list citing lack of adequate safety/efficacy data and impurity concerns

sources.title: Metabolic Pharmaceuticals withdraws AOD-9604 development program
sources.keyFindings: Australian regulatory documentation of development termination after Phase 2b/3 failure

sources.title: 2024 Prohibited List - Growth Hormone Fragments
sources.keyFindings: AOD-9604 listed under S2.5 Growth Hormone Releasing Factors and their releasing peptides; prohibited at all times

clinicalTrialFailure.outcome: Failed to meet primary endpoint for weight loss vs placebo

notes: AOD-9604 was developed in Australia by Metabolic Pharmaceuticals based on research identifying the lipolytic domain of human growth hormone. Despite promising preclinical results in rodent models, the Phase 2b/3 OPTIONS study (n=536) failed to demonstrate statistically significant weight loss compared to placebo in 2007. Development was discontinued. The peptide is now prohibited by WADA and excluded from FDA compounding lists. Species differences in beta-3 adrenergic receptor biology are cited as the likely reason for translation failure from rodents to humans.
